name: Sodium nitroprusside
indications:
## Main indications

Arterial and venous vasodilator.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Ampoule or vial for IV infusion per institutional presentation.
adulto.reconstitution: Reconstitute per package insert; dilute in 5% dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
adulto.dose: 0,3–10 mcg/kg/min titrated; maximum infusion time per protocol.
adulto.administration: Continuous IV infusion pump; central line preferred.
adulto.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
adulto.notes: Monitor HR, invasive BP, urine output and peripheral perfusion.

pediatrico.finalConcentration: Usual 200 mcg/mL. With fluid restriction, 1000 mcg/mL.
pediatrico.infusionRate: Continuous infusion pump only, according to BP.
pediatrico.dose: Initial: 0,5-1 µg/kg/min, maintenance: up to 4-6 µg/kg/min
pediatrico.notes: Dilute in 5% dextrose at a concentration of 0,2 mg/ml (maximum dilution concentration: 1 mg/ml); dilution lasts 24 h if kept protected from light.

stability:
## General

- Protect strictly from light; discard if solution blue/gray.

## Pediatric guide

- 24 h once reconstituted. 24 h once diluted.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Nausea, vomiting, sweating, restlessness, headache, palpitations. Thiocyanate intoxication.
